seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: output: keep the max-cut goal but tighten the output to a perfectly balanced bisection (exactly n/2 per side), turning easy unconstrained max-cut into NP-hard balanced max-bisection; instances are planted so a random reference split is far from optimal.
title: Glacier Sensor Net: Balanced Dual-Uplink Split
strategies: reference (id) split baseline: sensors 1..n/2 to station 0, rest to station 1
greedy constructive: place sensors in order, assign each to the station that maximizes cross-checked weight to already-placed sensors while respecting the n/2 capacity
local search: start from greedy and apply balance-preserving pair swaps that increase cross-checked weight (hill climbing with a swap budget)
planted-structure recovery / Kernighan-Lin style refinement to approach the hidden balanced bisection
